name: Oral Communication
description: GE Area 1C / A1: Oral Communication foundation requirement.

name: English Composition
description: GE Area 1A / A2: English Composition foundation requirement.

name: U.S. History
description: GE Area 4A / D1: U.S. History requirement, typically HIST 172 or HIST 173 for CS.

name: U.S. Constitution & American Ideals
description: GE Area 4B / D2: U.S. Constitution and American Ideals requirement, typically POSC 100 for CS.

name: Ethnic Studies
description: GE Area 6 / F: Ethnic Studies requirement.

name: Upper Division Social and Behavioral Sciences
description: Upper-division GE Area 4 UD / UD D requirement. Take after at least 60 completed units.
notes: One remaining upper-division GE slot after CECS 491A and ENGR 350 double-count

name: Mechanics and Heat
description: First course in introductory calculus-based physics. Kinematics, forces and Newton's laws, conservation of energy and momentum, collisions, angular momentum, engines, entropy.
notes: Physical science option that also satisfies GE Area 5A / B1 and 5C / B3

name: General Chemistry
description: First semester of a two-semester sequence covering chemical reactions, stoichiometry, thermochemistry, bonding, and the properties of matter.
notes: Physical science option. Catalog requires math preparation equivalent to MATH 112B or higher

name: Biology Elective
description: Choose one: BIOL 200 (General Biology), BIOL 205 (Human Biology), or BIOL 207 (Human Physiology).
notes: One biology course required (BIOL 200, 205, or 207). Check catalog for current options.

name: Introduction to the Engineering Profession
description: Freshman orientation seminar on careers in engineering and the opportunities and challenges in the profession.
notes: Engineering orientation course. Catalog allows lower math alternatives; CS path uses MATH 122

name: Academic Success Skills
description: Development of skills and identification of strengths and weaknesses for success in a COE major.

name: Computers, Ethics, and Society
description: Examination of the social impact of information technologies, ethical obligations, privacy, and legal issues related to computing.
notes: Double-counts as upper-division GE Area 3 UD / UD C

name: Scientific Research Communication
description: Technical writing and oral presentation for students pursuing research careers.
notes: Writing intensive option. Requires GE foundations and upper-division standing

name: Information and Communication Technology for Sustainability
description: Information and communication technology for sustainability, including systems thinking and climate-related engineering contexts.
notes: Writing intensive option. Requires GE foundations and upper-division standing

name: Probability and Statistics with Applications to Computing
description: Applications of probability and statistics to computing, including random variables, Markov chains, hypothesis testing, and regression.

name: Cybersecurity in Business
description: Business applications of cybersecurity, including risk assessment, assurance, internal controls, and security operations.
